title: Admin Dashboards V1 — Per-Role Widget Configuration
replanNote: Fully regenerated after spec §6 rewrite (2026-05-26). Phase 0.5 schema extension added (T-003/T-004). Tasks T-003..T-016 remapped. Old billing-revoke tasks removed. T-001/T-002 preserved as completed. UPDATED 2026-05-27: T-009 (comments listing) and T-011 (reviews-pending count) extracted to SPEC-165/SPEC-166 after Phase-1 scout found required tables do not exist. IDs tombstoned (status: extracted). T-012 set in-progress (parallel route construction task). Summary reflects 39 active tasks (41 IDs - 2 tombstones).

tasks.title: Confirm SPEC-154 exports and identify schema gaps
tasks.description:
Verify that `DashboardCardConfig`, widget Zod schema, and section renderer are available and exported from SPEC-154's config infrastructure. Identify the two schema gaps (`onMissing` missing from `WidgetSchema`, `'checklist'` missing from `WidgetTypeSchema`) and the stub reconciliation needed.

Files checked:
- `apps/admin/src/config/ia/` (SPEC-154 config schema exports)
- `apps/admin/src/components/sections/` or equivalent renderer
- `.claude/audit/admin-redesign/proposals/02-config-schema.md` §7

Acceptance criteria (AC-1, AC-3):
- `DashboardCardConfig` type is exported and includes `id`, `type`, `label` (tri-locale), `scope`, `source`.
- Zod schema exists that validates dashboard card configs at runtime.
- Two gaps documented: `onMissing` absent from `WidgetSchema`, `'checklist'` absent from `WidgetTypeSchema`.
- Stub reconciliation need documented: `adminDashboard` / `superAdminDashboard` → `adminBaseDashboard` + `superAdminOnlySection`.

**COMPLETED**: gaps confirmed, motivating Phase 0.5 tasks T-003 and T-004.

tasks.title: ADMIN billing blast-radius audit
tasks.description:
Enumerate every admin billing route and page; verify the real billing PermissionEnum entries; produce written finding.

Files audited:
- `apps/api/src/routes/admin/billing/`
- `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/billing/`
- `packages/seed/src/required/rolePermissions.seed.ts`
- `packages/schemas/src/enums/permission.enum.ts`

Acceptance criteria (§3 Permission changes):
- Written finding documents each billing route/page with its permission check.
- Finding confirms the real ADMIN billing perm set (6 entries: `BILLING_READ_ALL`, `BILLING_MANAGE`, `MANAGE_SUBSCRIPTIONS`, `BILLING_PROMO_CODE_READ`, `BILLING_PROMO_CODE_MANAGE`, `BILLING_METRICS_READ`).
- Finding confirms spec-named perms (`BILLING_METRICS_VIEW`, `SUBSCRIPTION_VIEW_ALL`) do NOT exist in PermissionEnum.
- Blast-radius scope extracted to SPEC-164. No seed change in SPEC-155.

**COMPLETED**: fictional perm names confirmed; 6 real perms identified; blast-radius scope extracted to SPEC-164.

tasks.title: Extend WidgetSchema with onMissing field and checklist type
tasks.description:
Extend `WidgetSchema` in `apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts` with the two additions required by SPEC-155 dashboard configs.

Files to modify:
- `apps/admin/src/config/ia/schema.ts` — add `onMissing` optional field to `WidgetSchema`; add `'checklist'` to `WidgetTypeSchema`
- Update Zod test suite (SPEC-154 CI validation) to assert both additions

Changes (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3):
1. Add optional `onMissing` field to `WidgetSchema` reusing `OnMissingSchema` (values: `'hide' | 'disable'`); default `'disable'`. `OnMissingSchema` already exists for sidebar items — reuse it without duplication.
2. Add `'checklist'` to `WidgetTypeSchema` alongside existing values (`kpi | list | chart | feed | callout | shortcut | map | calendar`).

Test requirements (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3):
- Zod test: `WidgetSchema.parse({ ...validWidget, onMissing: 'hide' })` succeeds
- Zod test: `WidgetSchema.parse({ ...validWidget, onMissing: 'disable' })` succeeds
- Zod test: existing config objects that omit `onMissing` continue to pass validation
- Zod test: `WidgetTypeSchema.parse('checklist')` succeeds
- Zod test: all pre-existing WidgetTypeSchema values still parse
- SPEC-154 CI suite must remain green after extension

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/protected/host/favorites/breakdown route
tasks.description:
Build a new protected API route returning per-accommodation bookmark counts scoped to the authenticated host.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/favorites-breakdown.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/index.ts`
- Uses existing `countBookmarksForEntity()` from `@repo/db` or service layer

Route spec (AC-20):
- `GET /api/v1/protected/host/favorites/breakdown`
- Auth: requires authenticated session with HOST role
- Scope: `ownerId` automatically injected from `ctx.user.id` — NOT from query params
- Response shape: `{ data: [{ accommodationId: string, slug: string, bookmarkCount: number }] }`
- Empty array if host has no accommodations

Test requirements (AC-20):
- Integration test: `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/__tests__/favorites-breakdown.test.ts`
- Test: authenticated HOST user returns only their own accommodations
- Test: response shape matches documented interface exactly
- Test: unauthenticated request returns 401
- Test: non-HOST role returns 403

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/protected/host/conversations/response-rate route
tasks.description:
Build a new protected API route computing conversation response rate and average response time, scoped to the authenticated host.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/conversations-response-rate.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/index.ts`
- Aggregates over `conversations` table fields: `ownerMessageCount`, `firstGuestMessageAt`, `firstOwnerReplyAt` where `ownerId = ctx.user.id`

Route spec (AC-21):
- `GET /api/v1/protected/host/conversations/response-rate`
- Auth: requires authenticated session with HOST role
- Scope: `ownerId` injected from auth context
- Response shape: `{ data: { responseRatePct: number, avgResponseTimeMinutes: number } }`
- `responseRatePct`: (conversations with owner reply / total conversations) * 100, rounded to 1 decimal
- `avgResponseTimeMinutes`: mean of (`firstOwnerReplyAt` - `firstGuestMessageAt`) for replied conversations
- Returns `{ responseRatePct: 0, avgResponseTimeMinutes: 0 }` when no conversations

Test requirements (AC-21):
- Integration test: `apps/api/src/routes/protected/host/__tests__/conversations-response-rate.test.ts`
- Test: correct pct and avg with seeded conversation data
- Test: returns zeros when no conversations exist
- Test: scope isolation — HOST A cannot see HOST B's rates
- Test: 401/403 for unauthenticated/wrong role

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/newsletter/subscribers/by-preference route
tasks.description:
Build a new admin API route aggregating active newsletter subscribers by content preference.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/admin/newsletter/subscribers-by-preference.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/admin/newsletter/index.ts`
- Aggregates over `newsletter_subscribers.preferences` JSONB column where `status = 'active'`

Route spec (AC-22):
- `GET /api/v1/admin/newsletter/subscribers/by-preference`
- Auth: requires `NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER_VIEW` permission
- Response shape: `{ data: { OFFERS: number, EVENTS: number, GUIDES: number, PRODUCT_NEWS: number } }`
- Counts subscribers whose preferences include each category (a subscriber can appear in multiple categories)
- Returns zeros for categories with no subscribers

Test requirements (AC-22):
- Integration test: `apps/api/src/routes/admin/newsletter/__tests__/subscribers-by-preference.test.ts`
- Test: correct counts per preference category with seeded subscribers
- Test: only active subscribers are counted
- Test: 403 without `NEWSLETTER_SUBSCRIBER_VIEW`
- Test: response shape matches documented interface

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/posts/trend route
tasks.description:
Build a new admin API route returning the post publication trend for the last 12 months.

Files to create/modify:
- `apps/api/src/routes/admin/posts/trend.ts` (new route handler)
- Register in `apps/api/src/routes/admin/posts/index.ts`
- Date-grouped aggregation over `posts.createdAt` for the last 12 months

Route spec (AC-23):
- `GET /api/v1/admin/posts/trend`
- Auth: requires `POST_VIEW` or equivalent admin permission
- Response shape: `{ data: [{ month: string, count: number }] }` where `month` is `'YYYY-MM'` format
- Array has exactly 12 entries covering the last 12 calendar months (including current)
- Months with zero posts return `{ month: 'YYYY-MM', count: 0 }`
- Ordered chronologically (oldest first)

Test requirements (AC-23):
- Integration test: `apps/api/src/routes/admin/posts/__tests__/trend.test.ts`
- Test: returns 12 entries
- Test: correct counts for months with seeded posts
- Test: zero-fill for months without posts
- Test: 403 without required permission

This work is now owned by SPEC-165 (post-event-comments-system). EDITOR dashboard card H renders a deferred placeholder until SPEC-165 ships.

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/moderation/pending-count route

Route spec (AC-25):
- `GET /api/v1/admin/moderation/pending-count`
- Auth: requires `MODERATION_REVIEW` permission
- Response shape: `{ data: { total: number, byEntity: { accommodations: number, destinations: number, posts: number, events: number } } }`
- `total` = sum of all four byEntity counts
- Each entity count is independently computed

Test requirements (AC-25):

- Test: correct totals per entity with seeded pending items
- Test: `total` equals sum of byEntity values

- Test: response shape matches documented interface

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/users/stats route
tasks.description:

Route spec (AC-25):
- `GET /api/v1/admin/users/stats`
- Auth: requires `USER_VIEW_ALL` permission
- Response shape: `{ data: { byRole: Record<string, number>, newUsersTrend: [{ month: string, count: number }] } }`

Test requirements (AC-25):

tasks.title: Integration tests for all 7 active aggregation routes (T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008, T-010, T-012, T-013)
tasks.description:
Write comprehensive integration tests for each of the 7 active aggregation routes. One test file per route, each asserting shape, authentication, and scope isolation. T-009 (comments) and T-011 (reviews-pending) were extracted to SPEC-165/SPEC-166 and are NOT included.

This task is a consolidation/hardening pass — each individual route task includes its own inline test requirements. T-014 ensures all 7 are green as a group and adds cross-cutting edge cases.

Acceptance criteria (AC-20 through AC-26):
- All 7 active routes pass their shape assertions
- All 7 active routes pass their auth assertions (401 unauthenticated, 403 wrong permission)
- HOST routes assert scope isolation (no cross-owner data leaks)
- Trend routes assert 12-entry arrays with correct `YYYY-MM` format
- All tests run in under 30s total

Test requirements:
- Use the project's standard integration test harness (Vitest + db test setup)
- AAA pattern throughout
- No `.only()` or hard-coded `.skip()` in committed code

tasks.title: Create dashboard source resolver skeleton
tasks.description:
Scaffold the `dashboard-sources.ts` registry with: role context provider hook, typed resolver interface, and query key factory. No actual source registrations yet — just the infrastructure.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/lib/dashboard-sources.ts` — main registry
- `apps/admin/src/contexts/DashboardContext.tsx` (or similar) — role/user context provider

Key types to define (AC-16, AC-19):
```typescript
type SourceId = string;
type DashboardContext = { userId: string; role: string; permissions: string[] };
type ResolvedSource = { queryKey: readonly unknown[]; queryFn: () => Promise<unknown> };
type SourceResolver = (ctx: DashboardContext) => ResolvedSource;
```

Query key convention (AC-19):
- All dashboard query keys must include role and scope: `['dashboard', role, sourceId, scopeId?]`
- 60s `staleTime` set as default in resolver output
- Refetch-on-focus enabled by default

Test requirements:
- Unit test: registry returns a resolver for a given sourceId
- Unit test: query key includes role identifier

tasks.title: Create DeferredWidget component
tasks.description:
Build a reusable `DeferredWidget` component that renders a 'coming soon' placeholder for any 🔴 data slot, without erroring and without blocking other slots in the same card.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/components/dashboards/widgets/DeferredWidget.tsx`

Component interface:
```typescript
interface DeferredWidgetProps {
  phaseSpec: string;       // e.g. 'SPEC-159'
  description: string;     // Human-readable description of what will appear here
  expectedLabel?: string;  // Optional: label of the future data point
}
```

Acceptance criteria (AC-14, AC-15):
- Renders a visually consistent 'coming soon' state
- Does NOT throw errors
- Does NOT block rendering of other slots in the same card
- Uses i18n-safe labels (es/en/pt)

Test requirements (AC-14):
- Unit test: renders without errors given valid props
- Unit test: displays phaseSpec reference in rendered output
- Unit test: renders independently of any data fetching (no query calls)

- Card B: Mi plan (status widget) — source: `host.plan.status`

tasks.title: Build dashboard renderer component
tasks.description:
Build the main dashboard renderer component that reads the current user's resolved config(s), fires all source queries in parallel, dispatches to the appropriate widget type renderers, and includes the 'Actualizar' button.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/components/dashboards/DashboardRenderer.tsx`

Behavior (AC-8, AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- Role resolution: ADMIN → `adminBaseDashboard`; SUPER_ADMIN → `adminBaseDashboard` + `superAdminOnlySection`; HOST → `hostDashboard`; EDITOR → `editorDashboard`
- Fires all source queries in parallel using TanStack Query's `useQueries` (no waterfall — AC-34)
- Each card renders in its own Suspense boundary so one slow card does not block others
- 'Actualizar' button calls `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['dashboard', role] })` (AC-33)
- Cards sharing the same TanStack Query prefix key re-use cached data (AC-35)
- Widget type dispatch: `kpi` → KpiWidget, `list` → ListWidget, `chart` → ChartWidget, `checklist` → ChecklistWidget, `status` → StatusWidget, deferred slot → DeferredWidget
- `onMissing: 'hide'` gating evaluated against current user's permissions

Test requirements (AC-8, AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- Unit test: ADMIN resolves to 7 cards
- Unit test: SUPER_ADMIN resolves to 9 cards
- Unit test: 'Actualizar' button triggers invalidation
- Unit test: cards without user permission are hidden when `onMissing: 'hide'`

tasks.title: HOST scope isolation tests

tasks.title: SUPER gating tests

tasks.title: Performance baseline tests
tasks.description:
Write tests asserting that all dashboard queries fire in parallel (no waterfall) and that initial render completes in under 500ms on a warm cache.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-performance.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- All queries for the current role's dashboard are initiated within the same render cycle (no waterfall)
- Initial render completes in < 500ms with warm cache
- Multiple cards querying the same underlying entity share a TanStack Query key prefix and do not make duplicate network requests

Test requirements (AC-33, AC-34, AC-35):
- Unit test: use `useQueries` spy to assert all queries initiated in a single render
- Unit test: measure time from mount to last query initiation — assert < 500ms
- Unit test: ADMIN cards A (entities count) and B (accommodations list) share `['dashboard', 'admin', 'accommodations']` prefix — assert no duplicate fetches
- Use vitest fake timers for the 500ms assertion

tasks.title: Deferred-placeholder rendering tests
tasks.description:
Write tests asserting that each 🔴 slot renders `DeferredWidget` without errors and that the remaining 🟢/🟡 slots in the same card are unaffected.

Files to create:
- `apps/admin/src/__tests__/dashboard-deferred.test.ts`

Acceptance criteria (AC-14):
- Every deferred (🔴) slot renders `DeferredWidget` component
- The `DeferredWidget` receives the correct `phaseSpec` prop for each slot:
  - HOST card G views slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-159'`
  - EDITOR card C open-rate slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-160'`
  - EDITOR card E views slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-159'`
  - EDITOR card F views slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-159'`
  - EDITOR card H (entire card) → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-165'`
  - ADMIN card D failed/last-run slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-161'`
  - ADMIN card F reviews-pending sub-slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-166'`
  - SUPER card H audit log slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-162'`
  - SUPER card H security log slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-162'`
  - SUPER card H Sentry slot → `phaseSpec: 'SPEC-163'`
- In each mixed card, the non-deferred slots render their data correctly alongside DeferredWidget
- No errors thrown when DeferredWidget is rendered

Test requirements (AC-14):
- Unit test per deferred slot: assert DeferredWidget rendered with correct phaseSpec
- Unit test: HOST card G live KPI slots render alongside deferred views slot
- Unit test: DeferredWidget renders in isolation without any query calls
